data.title: Navan, Inc. (NASDAQ: NAVN ) set to release Q3 earnings with estimated 17 cent per share loss and $182.09 million revenue. Shares fell 0.4% to $13.92.
data.description: Navan, Inc. (NASDAQ: NAVN) is scheduled to announce its third-quarter earnings after the market closes on Monday, December 15. Analysts are forecasting a loss of 17 cents per share for the Palo Alto, California-based company, with expected revenue of $182.09 million. In recent news, Navan secured a global partnership with Frasers Group for travel and expense management. Despite this, Navan's stock dipped by 0.4% to close at $13.92 on Thursday. For more details and analyst ratings, interested readers can find the complete story on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Dow sets record high, gains over 600 points. Fear & Greed Index moves to 'Neutral' zone with improving sentiment.
data.description: The market sentiment improved as the CNN Money Fear and Greed index shifted to the "Neutral" zone on Thursday. U.S. stocks performed well with the Dow Jones index rising by over 600 points, driven by a shift from pricey tech stocks to industrials, financials, and healthcare. The Federal Reserve's recent interest-rate cut bolstered overall risk appetite. Oracle Corp. (NASDAQ: ORCL) saw a drop of about 11% following disappointing earnings and guidance, while Nvidia Corp. (NASDAQ: NVDA) and Broadcom Inc. (NASDAQ: AVGO) each fell by 1.6%. Intel Corp also experienced a decline. More details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Cereno Scientific to attend JPM Week and Biotech Showcase in San Francisco on Jan. 12-18, 2026 - pioneering treatments for rare diseases.
data.description: In Gothenburg, Sweden on December 12, 2025, Cereno Scientific, listed on Nasdaq First North as CRNO B, a biotech company specializing in treatments for rare cardiovascular and pulmonary diseases to improve and prolong life, revealed its participation in JPM Week 2026...

data.title: Renowned sculptor Wu Weishan donates drafts of Nanjing Massacre memorial works ahead of National Memorial Day.
data.description: On December 12, 2025, in Beijing, China, the country is getting ready to honor the National Memorial Day for Nanjing Massacre Victims. Renowned sculptor Wu Weishan has generously contributed the original sketches of his significant thematic sculptures for the expansion initiative of the project. The sculptures hold international recognition and are a vital part of the remembrance event.

data.title: Floris Lagerwerf nominated as future CFO of IMCD starting January 2027, succeeding Hans Kooijmans.
data.description: Rotterdam, The Netherlands - IMCD N.V. has recently disclosed that Floris Lagerwerf is slated to take on the role of Chief Financial Officer (CFO) of the Company starting in January 2027. Lagerwerf's nomination by the Supervisory Board comes following the decision of the current CFO, Hans Kooijmans, to retire by the close of December 2026. IMCD N.V. is renowned globally as a premier partner in the distribution and formulation of specialty chemicals and ingredients.

data.title: OSB GROUP PLC reports purchase of 100,000 own shares.
data.description: A recent report reveals that the global smartphone market experienced a significant decrease in sales during the first quarter of 2021. Sales dropped by 11% compared to the previous year, with only 255 million units sold. The top five smartphone manufacturers were still able to maintain their market dominance, with Samsung leading the pack with a 23% market share. Apple followed closely behind with a 16% market share, while Xiaomi, Oppo, and Vivo rounded out the top five. Despite the overall decline in sales, the demand for 5G-enabled smartphones continued to grow, accounting for 37% of all smartphones sold in the quarter. Experts predict that the market may experience a rebound in the coming quarters as the global economy recovers from the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic.

data.title: Company buys Signifeye, expanding clinics network for ophthalmology.
data.description: In a recent report, it was revealed that the number of unemployed individuals in the country has increased by 1.5 million in the past month. The total unemployment rate now stands at 7.2%, marking a significant rise from the previous month. Additionally, the report highlights that the number of new job vacancies has decreased by 800,000, indicating a further strain on the job market. The current job vacancy rate is now at 3.5%, showcasing a challenging environment for job seekers. These statistics paint a concerning picture of the economic landscape and emphasize the need for targeted interventions to address the rising unemployment rate.

data.title: Tetragon Financial Group holding AGM on Dec 29, 2025. Closed-ended investment company in Guernsey.
data.description: London, December 12, 2025 - Tetragon will be conducting its yearly general meeting on December 29, 2025. The Notice of General Meeting can be accessed through a specified link. Tetragon is a closed-ended investment firm based in Guernsey, with a focus on non-voting shares.

data.title: GCL System Integration (GCL SI) is participating in COP30 to standardize global carbon credits, focusing on the GCL Carbon Data Platform.
data.description: In Suzhou, China, on December 12, 2025, GCL System Integration (GCL SI) announced its intensified efforts towards the development of the GCL Carbon Data Platform. This move comes in the wake of the COP30 climate conference in Brazil, which emphasized the importance of carbon credit standardization and traceability. The company aims to capitalize on global interest in these issues to further enhance its platform.

data.title: Federal Reserve board reappoints all 12 regional Bank presidents and first VPs, 'Trump-proofing' itself. Securing new 5-year terms starting March 1, 2026. Fed's Independence Protected.
data.description: The Federal Reserve Board unanimously approved the reappointment of all 12 regional Reserve Bank presidents and first vice presidents, ensuring their leadership for a new five-year term starting on March 1, 2026. The decision, although following a standard schedule, was made months before their tenure's expiration, drawing attention as a potential move to protect the central bank from political influence. University of Michigan economist Justin Wolfers suggested that this preemptive vote "Trump-proofed" the Fed. This move has sparked discussions among economic experts about the Fed's independence and its role in shaping monetary policies. The main focus of the reappointments seems to be securing the Fed's operational arm from external interference, emphasizing its autonomy in decision-making and economic policymaking.
